annotator_1.text: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.
annotator_1.source_quote: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.

annotator_1.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) is a method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.
annotator_1.source_quote: This paper introduces Cross-Context Review (CCR), a straightforward method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.

annotator_1.text: The experiment tested 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts) with 150 injected errors.
annotator_1.source_quote: We ran a controlled experiment: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts) with 150 injected errors

annotator_1.text: The experiment tested four review conditions: same-session Self-Review (SR), repeated Self-Review (SR2), context-aware Subagent Review (SA), and Cross-Context Review (CCR).
annotator_1.source_quote: tested under four review conditions -- same-session Self-Review (SR), repeated Self-Review (SR2), context-aware Subagent Review (SA), and Cross-Context Review (CCR).

annotator_1.text: Over 360 reviews, CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%.
annotator_1.source_quote: Over 360 reviews, CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%
CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming SR (24.6%, p=0.008, d=0.52)
CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming SR (24.6%, p=0.008, d=0.52), SR2 (21.7%, p<0.001, d=0.72)

annotator_1.text: Reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11).
annotator_1.source_quote: reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11)
CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure

annotator_1.text: CCR costs only one extra session.
annotator_1.source_quote: CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure, and costs only one extra session.

annotator_2.text: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.
annotator_2.source_quote: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.

annotator_2.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) is a method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.
annotator_2.source_quote: This paper introduces Cross-Context Review (CCR), a straightforward method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.

annotator_2.text: The experiment tested 30 artifacts consisting of code, technical documents, and presentation scripts.
annotator_2.source_quote: We ran a controlled experiment: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts)

annotator_2.text: 150 errors were injected into the 30 artifacts.
annotator_2.source_quote: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts) with 150 injected errors

annotator_2.text: The experiment tested four review conditions: same-session Self-Review (SR), repeated Self-Review (SR2), context-aware Subagent Review (SA), and Cross-Context Review (CCR).
annotator_2.source_quote: tested under four review conditions -- same-session Self-Review (SR), repeated Self-Review (SR2), context-aware Subagent Review (SA), and Cross-Context Review (CCR)

annotator_2.text: CCR achieved an F1 score of 28.6%.
SR achieved an F1 score of 24.6%.
SR2 achieved an F1 score of 21.7%.
SA achieved an F1 score of 23.8%.
CCR outperformed SR with statistical significance (p=0.008, d=0.52).
annotator_2.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming SR (24.6%, p=0.008, d=0.52)

annotator_2.text: CCR outperformed SR2 with statistical significance (p<0.001, d=0.72).
annotator_2.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming... SR2 (21.7%, p<0.001, d=0.72)

annotator_2.text: CCR outperformed SA with statistical significance (p=0.004, d=0.57).
annotator_2.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming... SA (23.8%, p=0.004, d=0.57)

annotator_2.text: Reviewing twice in the same session (SR2) did not significantly outperform reviewing once (SR) (p=0.11).
annotator_2.source_quote: reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11)

annotator_2.text: The lack of improvement from SR2 over SR rules out repetition as an explanation for CCR's advantage.
annotator_2.source_quote: The SR2 result matters most for interpretation: reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11), which rules out repetition as an explanation for CCR's advantage.

annotator_2.text: The benefit of CCR comes from context separation itself.
annotator_2.source_quote: The benefit comes from context separation itself.
CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure

annotator_2.text: CCR costs only one extra session.
annotator_2.source_quote: CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure, and costs only one extra session.
